attempts.response:
```spthy
theory Certification_Path_Token
begin

/* Modeled protocol:
   - Roles: A (sender/originator), B (receiver/recipient), CA (certification authority).
   - Primitives: hashing (h), digital signatures (sign/verify), public-key encryption (aenc/adec).
   - Message from A to B:
       < 'path', <pkA, certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA >
     where:
       pkA   = pk(skA) (A's public key),
       certA = sign(pkA, skCA) (certificate by CA over pkA),
       info  = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)>,
       sigA  = sign(h(info), skA) (A signs the hash of info).
   - B verifies the CA certificate, checks intended recipient, verifies A's signature,
     and decrypts the confidential payload.

   Security goals:
   - Confidentiality of encData: encData remains secret unless B's long-term key is revealed.
   - Authentication (B's viewpoint, non-injective): If an honest B accepts, then some A sent
     a matching token, unless A's long-term key is revealed.
*/

builtins: hashing, signing, asymmetric-encryption

// Role A: constructs a signed token containing non-confidential sgnData and
// confidential encData encrypted for B. Includes a certification path (CA->A).
let A(skA, pkB, pkCA) =
  in(certA);                                        // obtain A's certificate (public information)
  if verify(certA, pk(skA), pkCA) = true then       // check CA's signature on A's public key
    new rA;                                         // replay/freshness guard
    new tA;                                         // timestamp (modeled as fresh)
    new sgnData;                                    // non-confidential parameters
    new encData;                                    // confidential parameters (intended to remain secret)
    let info = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)> in
    let sigA = sign(h(info), skA) in                // A{Info} = Info, As[h(Info)]
    event Token(pk(skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
    event SecretData(encData);
    out(<'path', <pk(skA), certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA>)

// Role B: verifies the certification path and A's signature, checks intended recipient,
// and decrypts the confidential payload.
let B(skB, pkCA) =
  in(m);
  let <'path', <pkA, certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA> = m in
  if verify(certA, pkA, pkCA) = true then           // validate cert: pkA signed by CA
    let <tA, rA, pkB_in, sgnData, xenc> = info in
    if pkB_in = pk(skB) then                        // ensure B is the intended recipient
      if verify(sigA, h(info), pkA) = true then     // verify origin authentication & integrity
        let encData = adec(xenc, skB) in            // recover confidential parameters
        event AcceptB(pkA, pk(skB), tA, rA, sgnData, encData)
      else 0
    else 0
  else 0

// CA: issues certificates binding agents' public keys. The CA's public key is the trust anchor.
let CA(skCA) =
  out(pk(skCA));                                   // publish CA trust anchor
  !(
    in(pkA);                                       // receive a public key to certify
    let certA = sign(pkA, skCA) in
    out(certA)                                     // publish its certificate
  )

// Compromise actions (long-term key reveals)
let CompromiseA(skA) = event LtkRevealA(pk(skA)); out(skA)
let CompromiseB(skB) = event LtkRevealB(pk(skB)); out(skB)
let CompromiseCA(skCA) = event CAReveal(pk(skCA)); out(skCA)

// Orchestrate many As and Bs, anchored by one CA.
process:
new ~skCA;
(
  !CA(~skCA)
  |
  !(
    new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    (
      !A(skA, pk(skB), pk(~skCA))
      | !B(skB, pk(~skCA))
      | (CompromiseA(skA))
      | (CompromiseB(skB))
      | (CompromiseCA(~skCA))
    )
  )
)

// Executability: some run where B accepts a token sent by A.
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s e #i #j.
      Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
    & AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #j
    & #i < #j"

// Confidentiality of encData: encData remains secret unless B's long-term key is revealed.
lemma encData_secrecy:
  "not(
     Ex pkA pkB t r s e #i #j.
        Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
      & K(e) @ #j
      & not(Ex #rB. LtkRevealB(pkB) @ #rB)
   )"

// Authentication (B's viewpoint, non-injective):
// If an honest B accepts a token claimed to be from an honest A,
// then some matching token was sent by that A, unless A's key was revealed.
lemma auth_B_noninjective:
  "All pkA pkB t r s e #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
    & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ #hA)
    & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ #hB)
    ==>
       (Ex #j. Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #j & #j < #i)
     | (Ex #rA. LtkRevealA(pkA) @ #rA)
  "

end
```
attempts.code:
theory Certification_Path_Token
begin

/* Modeled protocol:
   - Roles: A (sender/originator), B (receiver/recipient), CA (certification authority).
   - Primitives: hashing (h), digital signatures (sign/verify), public-key encryption (aenc/adec).
   - Message from A to B:
       < 'path', <pkA, certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA >
     where:
       pkA   = pk(skA) (A's public key),
       certA = sign(pkA, skCA) (certificate by CA over pkA),
       info  = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)>,
       sigA  = sign(h(info), skA) (A signs the hash of info).
   - B verifies the CA certificate, checks intended recipient, verifies A's signature,
     and decrypts the confidential payload.

   Security goals:
   - Confidentiality of encData: encData remains secret unless B's long-term key is revealed.
   - Authentication (B's viewpoint, non-injective): If an honest B accepts, then some A sent
     a matching token, unless A's long-term key is revealed.
*/

builtins: hashing, signing, asymmetric-encryption

// Role A: constructs a signed token containing non-confidential sgnData and
// confidential encData encrypted for B. Includes a certification path (CA->A).
let A(skA, pkB, pkCA) =
  in(certA);                                        // obtain A's certificate (public information)
  if verify(certA, pk(skA), pkCA) = true then       // check CA's signature on A's public key
    new rA;                                         // replay/freshness guard
    new tA;                                         // timestamp (modeled as fresh)
    new sgnData;                                    // non-confidential parameters
    new encData;                                    // confidential parameters (intended to remain secret)
    let info = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)> in
    let sigA = sign(h(info), skA) in                // A{Info} = Info, As[h(Info)]
    event Token(pk(skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
    event SecretData(encData);
    out(<'path', <pk(skA), certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA>)

// Role B: verifies the certification path and A's signature, checks intended recipient,
// and decrypts the confidential payload.
let B(skB, pkCA) =
  in(m);
  let <'path', <pkA, certA>, 'msg1', info, sigA> = m in
  if verify(certA, pkA, pkCA) = true then           // validate cert: pkA signed by CA
    let <tA, rA, pkB_in, sgnData, xenc> = info in
    if pkB_in = pk(skB) then                        // ensure B is the intended recipient
      if verify(sigA, h(info), pkA) = true then     // verify origin authentication & integrity
        let encData = adec(xenc, skB) in            // recover confidential parameters
        event AcceptB(pkA, pk(skB), tA, rA, sgnData, encData)
      else 0
    else 0
  else 0

// CA: issues certificates binding agents' public keys. The CA's public key is the trust anchor.
let CA(skCA) =
  out(pk(skCA));                                   // publish CA trust anchor
  !(
    in(pkA);                                       // receive a public key to certify
    let certA = sign(pkA, skCA) in
    out(certA)                                     // publish its certificate
  )

// Compromise actions (long-term key reveals)
let CompromiseA(skA) = event LtkRevealA(pk(skA)); out(skA)
let CompromiseB(skB) = event LtkRevealB(pk(skB)); out(skB)
let CompromiseCA(skCA) = event CAReveal(pk(skCA)); out(skCA)

// Orchestrate many As and Bs, anchored by one CA.
process:
new ~skCA;
(
  !CA(~skCA)
  |
  !(
    new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    (
      !A(skA, pk(skB), pk(~skCA))
      | !B(skB, pk(~skCA))
      | (CompromiseA(skA))
      | (CompromiseB(skB))
      | (CompromiseCA(~skCA))
    )
  )
)

// Executability: some run where B accepts a token sent by A.
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s e #i #j.
      Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
    & AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #j
    & #i < #j"

// Confidentiality of encData: encData remains secret unless B's long-term key is revealed.
lemma encData_secrecy:
  "not(
     Ex pkA pkB t r s e #i #j.
        Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
      & K(e) @ #j
      & not(Ex #rB. LtkRevealB(pkB) @ #rB)
   )"

// Authentication (B's viewpoint, non-injective):
// If an honest B accepts a token claimed to be from an honest A,
// then some matching token was sent by that A, unless A's key was revealed.
lemma auth_B_noninjective:
  "All pkA pkB t r s e #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #i
    & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ #hA)
    & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ #hB)
    ==>
       (Ex #j. Token(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, e) @ #j & #j < #i)
     | (Ex #rA. LtkRevealA(pkA) @ #rA)
  "

end
